steps.title: Pin down the claim
steps.content: The banker is described as "very upset about something." The supporting quote must show visible emotional distress — tears, agitation, sleeplessness, etc.

steps.title: Find the quote that shows distress
steps.content: Choice D shows the banker lying on his bed while "his tears and emotion kept him for hours from sleeping." Tears plus hours of sleeplessness directly illustrate being very upset.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'cautiously broke the seals off the door': describes a careful action, not an emotional state; no distress shown. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It struck three o'clock': sets a quiet nighttime scene; says nothing about the banker's feelings. ✗
  • Choice C — 'delighted at the bet': shows the opposite emotion — delight, not upset. ✗
  • Choice D — 'his tears and emotion kept him for hours from sleeping': tears and sleeplessness clearly portray a banker who is very upset. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
